Category: Agriculture & Resources: Agriculture, Forestry, Fishing, Mining, Energy
Who is this for: Sustainability managers, ESG analysts, environmental compliance officers, sustainability coordinators, operations managers in agriculture and energy sectors, environmental reporting specialists, corporate responsibility managers
Team or Dept this is for: Sustainability, Environmental Compliance, Operations, Risk Management, Corporate Affairs, Legal, Agriculture Operations, Energy Operations
Description: Risks of relying on AI for ESG and environmental reporting.
Prerequisites: A basic familiarity with environmental or agricultural business operations, along with a general awareness of sustainability reporting concepts such as ESG frameworks, is all that is needed to get started with this course.
Learning objectives: By the end of this course, you will be able to identify the key risks associated with using AI tools for ESG and environmental reporting within agriculture, forestry, fishing, mining, and energy sectors, and explain how inaccurate or biased AI outputs can undermine sustainability metrics. You will also apply practical frameworks to evaluate and verify AI-generated data, ensuring your organisation's environmental reporting remains credible, transparent, and aligned with recognised sustainability standards.
